summary: Executed backend auth/dashboard/query regression via pytest against runtime API and validated frontend login-to-dashboard integration with Playwright. Core requested flows passed.

minor.issue: Supervisor backend/frontend programs are misconfigured to non-existent paths (/app/backend and /app/frontend spawn errors), so managed services are not usable in this workspace.

issues: Recharts logs width/height -1 warning in browser console on initial render (possible container sizing race).

action_items: Fix supervisor program working directories/commands so backend and frontend start reliably without manual workaround.
Align frontend backend URL env strategy with deployment contract (use env only, remove hardcoded localhost fallback).
Investigate Recharts container sizing warning on dashboard initial render.
critical_code_review_comments: frontend/src/services/api.js uses import.meta.env.VITE_BACKEND_URL plus hardcoded fallback http://127.0.0.1:8000; this violates strict env-only URL rule and can break production routing.
Repository service bootstrap configuration is inconsistent with actual structure (no /app/backend app folder), causing supervisor FATAL state and preventing standard runtime startup.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Manual runtime workaround used for this iteration: frontend via yarn dev on :5173 and backend via python /app/api.py on :8000 because supervisor programs fail to spawn. Auth/login, auth/me, dashboard/overview, query, and UI login/dashboard flows are validated as passing under this workaround.
rca of the issue: Root cause for service bring-up failures: supervisor config references /app/backend and legacy frontend start command, but this repo places backend entrypoint at /app/api.py and frontend as Vite app in /app/frontend with dev command. Reproduction: run `supervisorctl status` => backend/frontend FATAL with ENOENT and "error Command start not found" in logs. Mitigation: update supervisor program cwd/command to real project layout and Vite scripts, then retest with managed services only.
